structuredContent.nextAction: Call centian.task_complete_onboarding to freeze the onboarding context.

content.text:
MyOrgAPI — Create User Endpoint

POST /users

The endpoint accepts a JSON payload with the following structure:

{
  "tenant_id": string,
  "user": {
    "email": string,
    "display_name": string,
    "role": string (optional)
  },
  "metadata": {
    "source": "api",
    "version": integer
  }
}

Rules

  1. The email field must be lowercased before submission, except when the tenant_id starts with legacy_. Legacy tenants preserve the original casing for backwards compatibility.
  2. The role field must be omitted from the payload (not set to null, not set to any default value) when the tenant is on the free plan. Free-plan tenants are identified by tenant_id values starting with free_. This rule applies regardless of whether the caller provides a role value — any provided role is discarded for free-plan tenants. For all other tenants, role defaults to "member" if not specified by the caller.
  3. The display_name field must be trimmed of leading and trailing whitespace, with any sequence of internal whitespace collapsed to a single space.
  4. The metadata.version field must be 2 by default, but 1 for any tenant whose ID starts with archive_.

onboarding.taskSummary: Fix a MyOrgAPI payload bug in user_payload.py using Centian guided TDD. The API docs say free-plan tenants (tenant_id starting with free_) must omit user.role entirely from POST /users payloads, regardless of caller input. Existing implementation always includes role, which likely causes the reported 400. Relevant files are user_payload.py, existing regression coverage in test_user_payload.py, and a new regression test file test_regression.py.

artifactMap.notes: Existing regression coverage; must remain unchanged.
New regression file to add for the free-plan role omission bug.

commonCommands.purpose: Run the new regression test file.
Run the full standard-library unittest suite.

constraints: Use Centian workflow as source of truth.
Use get_docs before deciding behavior to test.
Write the failing regression first in test_regression.py.
Use only Python standard-library tooling already available.
Treat the project root as the working directory and use project-relative paths.

planning.planSummary: Use guided TDD against the documented free-plan role omission rule. First add a focused failing unittest regression in test_regression.py for a free-plan tenant with an explicit role, then implement the minimal change in user_payload.py so role is omitted only for free-plan tenants while existing behaviors remain intact. Finish with targeted regression, full unittest suite, and a py_compile validation pass.
invariants: Regression behavior must follow the docs: free-plan tenant IDs start with free_ and user.role must be omitted entirely regardless of input.
Use only standard-library Python test tooling.

```diff
Index: benchmarks/context_sprawl_v1/results/baseline_no_noise/20260515204311_current_codex_run/runs/current_codex_bug_fix_v1_attempt_003/project/user_payload.py
===================================================================
--- benchmarks/context_sprawl_v1/results/baseline_no_noise/20260515204311_current_codex_run/runs/current_codex_bug_fix_v1_attempt_003/project/user_payload.py	original
+++ benchmarks/context_sprawl_v1/results/baseline_no_noise/20260515204311_current_codex_run/runs/current_codex_bug_fix_v1_attempt_003/project/user_payload.py	modified
@@ -7,16 +7,18 @@
         "tenant_id": tenant_id,
         "user": {
             "email": user["email"].lower(),
             "display_name": " ".join(user["display_name"].split()),
-            "role": user.get("role", "member"),
         },
         "metadata": {
             "source": "api",
             "version": 1 if tenant_id.startswith("archive_") else 2,
         },
     }
 
+    if not tenant_id.startswith("free_"):
+        payload["user"]["role"] = user.get("role", "member")
+
     # Legacy tenants preserve email casing for backwards compatibility.
     if tenant_id.startswith("legacy_"):
         payload["user"]["email"] = user["email"]
 
```
